content: Just as human language learners deepen conceptual mastery by explicitly practicing reflexion (reflection) on writing outputs to spot mismatches between intent and expression, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow the structured language pedagogy framework of high school writing workshops to improve their ability to critique and refine their own outputs. The graded, iterative reflective exercises used to teach human writing can be repurposed to train weaker LLMs to self-reflect more effectively than current ad-hoc self-reflection prompts.
